content.text:
Here's the lay of the land in `CoX-mod-Adventure`:

| Item | What it is |
|------|-----------|
| `coh-modders/CoHModdingTool.dmg` | The Mac modding tool installer (this is the same DMG that got auto-mapped as Wine's `e:` drive earlier) |
| `coh-modders/CityModInstaller.zip` | The CityModInstaller package (zipped, not yet extracted) |
| `coh-setup.txt` (40k) | A transcript export of that earlier Antigravity/Gemini session where the Wine/CoH install was first reviewed |
| `.claude/settings.local.json` | Project-local Claude settings |

So this is your **modding staging area** — two unopened mod-tool archives (431 MB total) sitting next to the notes from the install review. And we already know from the runtime work that the game's mod drop-point — `~/Library/Application Support/LaunchCat/coh/assets/mods/` — is **empty and wired into the launch line** (`-assetpath assets\mods`), ready to receive content.

Before I start unpacking or installing anything, I want to aim at the right target.
